REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, PLAINTIFF BAYSIDE LASSEN, LLC, A DELAWARE LIMITED LIABILITY COMPANY, DOING BUSINESS IN CALIFORNIA AS BAYSIDE 441 LASSEN, LLC MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR, IN THE ALTERNATIVE, SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS DENIED. SUMMARY JUDGMENT/ADJUDICATION AS TO ASIAN'S COMPLAINT IS DENIED WITHOUT PREJUDICE. IT IS MOOT IN LIGHT OF THE RULING ON ASIAN'S MOTION FOR LEAVE TO FILE AN AMENDED COMPLAINT. SUMMARY ADJUDICATION ON BAYSIDE'S COMPLAINT IS DENIED FOR FAILURE TO COMPLY WITH CRC 3.1350(B). SUMMARY JUDGMENT ON BAYSIDE'S COMPLAINT IS DENIED. MOVING PARTY FAILED TO ESTABLISH EVERY ELEMENT OF THE CAUSE OF ACTION FOR BREACH OF CONTRACT. MOVING PARTY QUOTES A CONTRACTUAL PROVISION, BUT FAILS TO SHOW HOW ASIAN'S "ACTION" FITS INTO THE REQUIREMENTS/OBLIGATIONS OF THAT PROVISION AND WHAT THE DAMAGES ARE. IT ALSO APPEARS THAT THE MOVING PARTY IS ATTEMPTING TO ASSERT A CAUSE OF ACTION BASED ON BREACH OF COVENANT OF GOOD FAITH AND FAIR DEALING. THAT IS NOT THE CAUSE OF ACTION THAT IS ASSERTED IN THE COMPLAINT AS THE FIRST CAUSE OF ACTION FOR WHICH THE MOVING PARTY SEEKS SUMMARY JUDGMENT/ADJUDICATION. IT IS ALSO UNCLEAR WHAT THE DAMAGES ARE FOR BREACH OF THIS PROVISION AS THE SEPARATE STATEMENT PRESENTS NO EVIDENCE OF ANY NON-SPECULATIVE DAMAGES THAT MAY BE REDUCED TO JUDGMENT. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE ORDER IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312(B). JUDGE: CHARLES F. HAINES; CLERK: VANESSA WU; REPORTED, ANGELA POURTABIB CSR 13714, 415-601-7054, APOURTABIB@GMAIL.COM.(D501)

MINI MINUTES: BAYSIDE LASSEN, LLC'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION ON ITS DECLARATORY RELIEF CAUSE OF ACTION

Appearances: Paul Hesse of Messner Reeves (949.612.9128) and Steven Morger of Fennemore LLP (510.834.6600) for defendant Asian, Inc.; Alicia Vaz of Cox Castle & Nicholson LLP (310.284.2200) for plaintiff Bayside Lassen, LLC.

Argued and the court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: GRANTED as to Issue Two and DENIED as to Issue One and Issue Three.

Summary adjudication is denied as to Issue One because there are multiple reasonable readings of whether the redemption structure of Bayside's purchase complied with the terms of the option. The Court's prior ruling on the lis pendens motion is not dispositive on this issue because this motion has different arguments, evidence, and burdens on the parties.

Summary adjudication is appropriate as to Issue Two because all three conditions precedent needed to occur for Asian's Right of First Refusal to be valid, and Asian did not dispute Bayside's Undisputed Material Facts 21-26.

Summary adjudication is denied as to Issue Three because there are multiple reasonable readings of Section 8.01(b) and Section 8.02(b)(i). The Court notes that moving party failed to offer an analysis of these provisions aside from what was in the separate statement. Section 8.01(b) appears to operate "not in limitation" of the contract's other provisions only when the General Partners are "acting unanimously" and in service of the "purpose of the Partnership." It is unclear how Section 8.01(b) functions otherwise because it is implausible to think that the General Partners could be "acting unanimously" while they "cannot agree" on a matter. Prevailing party to submit a proposed form of order in compliance with CRC 3.1312. Judge: Garrett L. Wong, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Reporter: Peggy Tsujimoto, CSR 5229 (T: 415.828.1919, E: peggytsujimoto@gmail.com) (501/GLW)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Notice Of Motion And Motion For Summary Judgment Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support
Off Calendar Jul-30-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: THE BAYSIDE AND LASSEN PARTIES' MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR, IN THE ALTERNATIVE SUMMARY ADJUDICATION

Appearances: Paul Hesse of Messner Reeves (949.612.9128) and Steven Morger of Fennemore LLP (510.834.6600) for defendant Asian, Inc.; Alicia Vaz of Cox Castle & Nicholson LLP (310.284.2200) for plaintiff Bayside Lassen, LLC.

Argued and the court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: GRANTED. Moving party shifts the burden on all issues and Asian failed to raise a triable issue of fact.

It is undisputed that the conditions precedent to Asian's ROFR did not take place, which makes summary adjudication appropriate for Issue One, Issue Two, and part of Issue Three. Further, a three-year statute of limitations applies because the undisputed facts show that Mr. Chan knew Bayside Lassen was named in the agreement, and that his misunderstanding was not the result of fraud or forgery. The statute of limitations did not begin running in 2023, when Asian attempted to exercise its ROFR, because the conditions precedent for Asian's ROFR did not occur and thus no injury occurred in 2023. This statute of limitations makes summary adjudication appropriate for Issue Four, Issue Five, Issue Six, Issue Seven, and part of Issue Three.

As to the remainder of Issue Three, Asian identifies seven possible issues of fact, none of which are triable. Bayside's Undisputed Material Facts 35-40 are undisputed and demonstrate that Mr. Chan knew Bayside Lassen was named in the agreement and that his misunderstanding was not the result of fraud or forgery. Additionally, Asian's claim that Mr. Chan was told there would be "no financial (or other) impact on Asian" comes from Additional Material Fact Q. This fact is only substantiated by an improper Request for Judicial Notice; the Court cannot accept the truths of declarations filed in other cases. Moreover, Bayside cannot be held liable for a breach of fiduciary duty or for fraud by failing to make a representation about the redemption structure, the claimed injury of which goes to an invalid ROFR. Finally, as pled, causes of action 3 and 4 do not allege anything pertaining to representations to avoid transfer taxes, marketing the property, or diverting funds after Asian exercised its ROFR. Prevailing party to submit a proposed form of order in compliance with CRC 3.1312. Judge: Garrett L. Wong, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Reporter: Peggy Tsujimoto, CSR 5229 (T: 415.828.1919, E: peggytsujimoto@gmail.com) (501/GLW)

Ms. Vaz contests to the tentative. After reviewing the pleadings and listening to oral arguments the Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF BAYSIDE LASSEN, LLC, A DELAWARE LIMITED LIABILITY COMPANY, DOING BUSINESS IN CALIFORNIA AS BAYSIDE 441 LASSEN, LLC MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR, IN THE ALTERNATIVE, SUMMARY ADJUDICATION is DENIED. Summary Judgment/Adjudication as to Asian's complaint is denied without prejudice. It is moot in light of the ruling on Asian's motion for leave to file an amended complaint. Summary Adjudication on Bayside's complaint is DENIED for failure to comply with CRC 3.1350(b). Summary Judgment on Bayside's complaint is DENIED. Moving party failed to establish every element of the cause of action for breach of contract. Moving party quotes a contractual provision, but fails to show how Asian's "action" fits into the requirements/obligations of that provision and what the damages are. It also appears that the moving party is attempting to assert a cause of action based on breach of covenant of good faith and fair dealing. That is not the cause of action that is asserted in the complaint as the first cause of action for which the moving party seeks summary judgment/adjudication. It is also unclear what the damages are for breach of this provision as the Separate Statement presents no evidence of any non-speculative damages that may be reduced to judgment.

Prevailing party to prepare order in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b). Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: Vanessa Wu; Reported, Angela Pourtabib csr 13714, 415-601-7054, apourtabib@gmail.com.(D501)
